reasoning: Decoded independently, not taken from pair 5's MI-009. RBARON.MAC:2399-2408 verbatim: `PLNZD0: LDX I,10 / LDA OBJKLD+1 / BNE 2$ / LDA OBJKLD / LSR / CMP I,10 / BCS 2$ / TAX / 2$: LDA AX,PLNLVL / STA GMLEVL`. I explicitly tested the alternative reading — that the LSR is an LSR-as-bit-test idiom, which the SAME routine uses nine lines later (:2412-2416 `LDA N.PLNZ / LSR / LDA I,0 / BCS 5$` — there A is discarded and only C is used). It is NOT that here: at :2406 the shifted accumulator is consumed by `TAX` and used as the table index. The LSR is a genuine divide-by-two. Radix: line 2403 sits between `.RADIX 16` (:74) and the only decimal island (:6217), so `LDX I,10` and `CMP I,10` are 0x10 = 16 — corroborated by PLNLVL (:2478) having exactly 17 entries (0..16), which only makes sense with a 0x10 clamp. Arithmetic checks out: PLNLVL = [0,0,0,0,1,2,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4,4,4,5]; at 8 kills ROM index 8>>1=4 -> PLNLVL[4]=1, ours PLNLVL[8]=3; GMLEVL 5 needs index 16 -> OBJKLD>=32 in ROM, 16 in ours. Ours (scoring.ts:91) indexes by the raw count, and scoring.ts IS live (main.ts:44 imports gmlevlForKills, called at :389 and :467). GMLEVL's consumers are as cited (weave limits :2756, SHOOTS promotion :3024, ground fire :4038). Divergence is real: our difficulty ramps 2x too fast.
corrections: SCOPE — OBJKLD is not a pure kill count, so `objkld >> 1` alone will still not reproduce the ROM's ramp. LVLSTP (RBARON.MAC:2469-2474) is the ONLY writer, and it has FOUR call sites: :5584 (plane kill), :5651 (drone kill), :3904 (GROUND/playfield object kill, alongside PFSCRE scoring) and :1416 INITBP (`JSR LVLSTP ;STEP LEVEL FOR EACH BLIMP`) — which is the blimp SPAWN path, not a blimp kill. Our `kills` (main.ts:338, bumped at :427 and :439) counts blimp KILLS and plane/drone kills, and counts no ground objects at all. To match, the counter must bump on ground-object kills and on blimp APPEARANCE, not blimp death.

LVLSTP is gated by `BIT GAMODE / BPL` — OBJKLD only advances in play mode (attract mode seeds it to a fixed 10. at :2056). Irrelevant to us (no attract mode) but do not port the guard as a kill filter.

The finding says the clamp is 'to 0x10 = 16'. Precisely: `LDX I,10` presets the index to 16 and `BCS 2$` saturates there, so OBJKLD>=32 (or OBJKLD+1 nonzero) pins GMLEVL at PLNLVL[16]=5. Ours already clamps to PLNLVL.length-1 = 16, so ONLY the missing >>1 needs fixing on the index itself.

reasoning: Attacked hard on radix and BCD per the brief; it held, and a fourth independent witness turned up that the finding did not cite. Decode of PLNSCR (RBARON.MAC:3038-3050) verbatim: `LDA PLVALU / LDX PLSTAT+5 / CPX I,10 / BCC NWSCRE ;PLANE PAST BONUS DEPTH / DRNSCR: SOUND 3 / JSR SNDON / LDA I,DRNPNT ;XTRA POINTS IF DIM / NWSCRE: CMP I,DRNPNT ...`. BCC = branch if X < 0x10, so depth MSB BELOW 16 keeps A = PLVALU; depth MSB AT/ABOVE 16 falls through and loads the flat DRNPNT. PLSTAT+5 is unambiguously the depth MSB (:2322-2323 `LDA I,P.INDP/100 ;Z DEPTH (MSB) / STA PLSTAT+5`). Depth SHRINKS on approach: PLPOSZ (:2482) is `.BYTE -4,-10,-20,...,-80`, all NEGATIVE, stored to PLSTAT+1B ';PLANE MOTION DEPTH DELTA' (:2410-2411), and :2723-2726 tests against P.MNDP ';PLANE MIN. DEPTH' with BPL ';NOT YET'. So proportional-to-depth = proportional-to-FAR, exactly as the finding argues, and our scoring.ts:65 (DRONE_SCORE + (P_INDP - depth)*VALFRC) is inverted. PLVALU's arithmetic verified end-to-end: :2710-2721 `LDA PLSTAT+5 / LDX VALFRC / JSR MULTPL / TXA / STY TEMP2 / JSR DIVBY4 / JSR DIVBY4 / LDA TEMP2 / CMP VALMIN / BCS 55$ / LDA VALMIN / 55$: STA PLVALU`; MULTPL (:6063-6088) returns the product in X:Y, and DIVBY4 (:6170-6176) is two ROR-through-TEMP2 shifts, so two calls = >>4 = /16 exactly as claimed. SCORE ENCODING PROVEN, not assumed: DRNPNT = 30. (:508) carries an EXPLICIT trailing period = decimal 30; SCOREM (:1555-1576) drains SCRTAB+3 in binary while adding to SCORE under SED in units of 1 and 0x10, so SCORE is 16-bit BCD holding score/10 — the implicit trailing zero. 30 -> 300 points. It is NOT 0x300 and NOT BCD 3000. Confirmed twice more: NWSCRE (:3047) fires message 14. = the '300 POINT MESSAGE' only when the score equals DRNPNT, and the on-screen plane-value HUD (:5296-5302, which the finding never found) runs the IDENTICAL test — `LDA PLVALU / JSR DECIMAL / LDX PLSTAT+5 / CPX I,10 / BCC 5$ / LDA I,30 ;PLANE BONUS VALUE / STA NUMB1` — overriding the displayed value with packed-BCD 0x30 (= '30', i.e. 300) when the plane is at/beyond the threshold, and labelling that case the BONUS. The far/dim plane is the bonus; the knife fight pays less. Cap check: PLVALU <= max(VALMIN, 15*24/16=22) = 24 -> 240 points, always < DRNPNT's 300, so 300 IS the plane's ceiling. Ours peaks at 300 + (1080-140)*0.7 ~ 958 and would reach 1056 at depth 0. Direction inverted, ceiling ~3.5x too high. Real.
corrections: VALFRC's initial value is 7/0x10 = 7/16 = 0.4375, NOT 0.7. Our `VALFRC = 0.7` (scoring.ts:48) is a DECIMAL misreading of the hex comment at :5965 (';INITIALLY 7/10*DEPTH PLANE SCORE') — in a .RADIX 16 region that '/10' is /0x10 = /16, and the code confirms it with the two DIVBY4 calls (>>4). The equate comment at :124 ('* VALFRC/10') says the same thing. Do not carry 0.7 into the fix.

VALMIN starts at ZERO, not 10. It is cleared by the game-start wipe at :5959-5963 (`LDA I,0 / LDX I,EAWRTE-OBJKLD / STA ZX,OBJKLD` — VALMIN sits at :131, inside that span) and only VALFRC is then seeded to 7 (:5964). So the finding's 'nose-to-nose ... floored to VALMIN -> as little as 10 points' is wrong: point-blank on wave 1 a plane is worth 1*7>>4 = 0 -> max(0, VALMIN=0) = ZERO points. The floor only becomes meaningful after several waves ramp VALMIN (see CB-007).

The 'bonus depth' threshold is NOT a generous far band — it is essentially 'at or beyond SPAWN depth'. P.INDP = 1080 sits in the .RADIX 16 region directly beneath `L.OBJ = 28.` (which uses an explicit period), so it is 0x1080 = 4224, and :2322 spawns the plane with Z MSB = P.INDP/0x100 = 0x10 = 16 — EXACTLY the value PLNSCR tests against. The flat 300 is therefore reachable only at spawn depth or on the fly-by/recede (P.MNDM = 0x11 = 17, ';PLANE MAX DEPTH*(100) (FOR FLY BY)'). Everything else on the approach (MSB 1..15) pays PLVALU.

CONSEQUENCE FOR THE FIX: our P_INDP = 1080 (enemy.ts:41) and MIN_DEPTH = 140 (enemy.ts:63) are THEMSELVES decimal misreads of hex P.INDP = 0x1080 = 4224 and P.MNDP = 0x140 = 320. The finding's proposed `score = 300 when depth >= bonus threshold` cannot be implemented against our depth scale without first settling that scale. Express the fix on the ROM's Z-MSB scale (MSB = depth>>8, range 1..16), or the threshold will land in the wrong place.

MINOR — the corroboration at :5241-5247 is DSPSHL's SHELL intensity (it reads SHELLS+5, the shell's Z MSB), not the plane's. It does establish greater-depth = dimmer, so the inference survives, but it is not the plane's own intensity ramp as the finding's wording implies. The far stronger corroboration is the plane-value HUD at :5296-5302 (';PLANE BONUS VALUE'), which the finding missed.

reasoning: The column-wise table decode is the crux and it is not merely right, it is PROVABLE from the BCD requirement — which is the check the brief demanded. SCOREM (RBARON.MAC:1582-1602) verbatim: `LDA OPTION / AND I,0C / LSR / LSR / CLC / ADC EXLIFE / TAX / LDA SCORE+1 / CMP AX,BONUSL / BCC 30$ / CLC / LDA EXLIFE / ADC I,4 / STA EXLIFE ;EXTRA LIFE / SOUND 2 / JSR SNDON / ... / INC LIVES`. EXLIFE steps by 4, so X walks the 16-byte table in strides of 4 from the option index — i.e. in COLUMNS. Table (:1605-1608): row0 `2,4,6,0FF` / row1 `10,15,20,0FF` / row2 `30,40,50,0FF` / row3 `0FF x4`. RADIX PROOF: these bytes are compared against SCORE+1, which is a BCD byte (SCOREM adds under SED at :1565-1571, draining the binary SCRTAB+3 counter in units of 1 and 0x10). A BCD byte NEVER takes a value with a nibble in 0xA..0xF. Read as DECIMAL, BONUSL would contain 15 -> 0x0F and 20 -> 0x14 — thresholds a BCD SCORE+1 can never equal or exceed cleanly, and the table would be nonsense. Read as HEX (which the region is: .RADIX 16 at :74, no island before :6217) every entry — 0x02,0x04,0x06,0x10,0x15,0x20,0x30,0x40,0x50 — is a VALID packed BCD number. The hex reading is forced. So: SCORE is BCD score/10, SCORE+1 is the high digit-pair, and column 0 = 0x02/0x10/0x30 -> 2,000/10,000/30,000; column 1 = 0x04/0x15/0x40 -> 4,000/15,000/40,000; column 2 = 0x06/0x20/0x50 -> 6,000/20,000/50,000; column 3 = all 0FF -> never (SCORE+1 maxes at 0x99). Exactly as the finding decoded. Max three extra planes; EXLIFE starts at 0 (cleared by the :5959-5963 wipe). `INC LIVES` at :1602 is unambiguous. On our side I grepped `src/` myself rather than trusting the auditor: lives.ts exposes ONLY initialLives / loseLife / tickGrace — `count` has no increment path anywhere, and main.ts (:342, :406) only ever calls initialLives() and loseLife(). No BONUSL table, no EXLIFE, no score-threshold check. The mechanic is genuinely absent.
corrections: SCOPE — 'Extra lives (BONUSL) do not exist in our game at all' is OVERSTATED. The bonus-life EVENT and SOUND already exist and are wired: `BonusLifeEvent` with `type: 'bonus-life'` (src/core/events.ts:50-51, in the Event union at :59), the SOUND 2 rising-warble voice (src/shell/pokey.ts:124), and the dispatch case (src/shell/audio-dispatch.ts:61), with tests at tests/shell/audio-dispatch.test.ts:134,160. What is missing is only the AWARD LOGIC — nothing in src/ ever EMITS `{type:'bonus-life'}`, and nothing ever increments Lives.count. This materially shrinks the fix (size 'm' -> nearer 's'): the audio half is done; add the BONUSL table + EXLIFE cursor + the SCOREM threshold check, increment count, and emit the event that already exists.

The bonus check runs on every SCORE-DRAIN tick, not once per kill. SCOREM is invoked from the NMI (RBGRND.MAC:236-237) and drains SCRTAB+3 gradually under SCRTIM, so the threshold is re-tested as the score animates upward. Functionally equivalent to checking after each award, but do not port it as a per-kill hook if the score display is animated.
OUR score is raw points (300/200/...), not BCD score/10. The BONUSL thresholds therefore map to our score DIRECTLY as 2000/10000/30000 etc. — do not re-divide by 10 when porting the table.

reasoning: Read on its own terms and it holds; the finding even pre-empted the radix trap in its own reasoning and got it right. STPLNE (RBARON.MAC:2274-2285) verbatim: `STPLNE: BIT GAMODE / BPL 15$ ;NOT PLAY MODE / LDY I,1 / 5$: LDX ZY,VALFRC / INX / CPX I,19 / BCC 10$ / LDX I,18 / 10$: STX ZY,VALFRC / DEY / BPL 5$`. Y runs 1 then 0, so the loop bumps BOTH bytes: Y=1 -> VALFRC+1, and VALMIN = VALFRC+1 is established at :131 (`VALMIN =VALFRC+1 ;PLANE VALUE MINIMUM`). Radix verified in the hex region (:74 .RADIX 16, island only at :6217): `CPX I,19` = 0x19 = 25, `LDX I,18` = 0x18 = 24 — so the finding's stated cap of 24 (NOT 25, NOT 0x19-as-decimal) is exactly right; it did not fall for its own trap. Entry point verified: :2270-2273 `LDA MODECT / LSR / BCC STPLNE ;PLANE WAVE / JMP INITGR ;GROUND WAVE` — STPLNE is the plane-wave initializer, so the ramp is one step per plane wave, gated to play mode. Initial values verified: VALFRC = 7 at :5964-5965, VALMIN = 0 via the game-start wipe at :5959-5963 (VALMIN at :131 falls inside OBJKLD..EAWRTE). VALMIN's use as the floor confirmed at :2718-2720 (`CMP VALMIN / BCS 55$ / LDA VALMIN`). Late-game arithmetic checks out: at VALMIN=VALFRC=24, max depth-derived PLVALU = 15*24>>4 = 22 < 24, so every close plane pays exactly the floor, 240 points. Ours (scoring.ts:48) is a single fixed `const VALFRC = 0.7`, with no VALMIN and no per-wave ramp anywhere in src/. The mechanic is absent.
corrections: The finding leaves the reader with '0.7' unchallenged as the current value's provenance. State it plainly in the fix: the ROM's initial VALFRC is 7 applied as 7/0x10 = 7/16 = 0.4375, because the divide is two DIVBY4 calls (>>4) at :2715-2716 and the source comments ('/10' at :124 and :5965) are HEX. Our 0.7 is a decimal misread of that comment, so the ramp's START value is wrong as well as its absence — porting the ramp onto a 0.7 base would compound the error.

The ramp is per PLANE WAVE, and STPLNE is reached only on the plane-wave branch of the MODECT alternation (:2270-2273) — plane and GROUND waves alternate. So VALFRC/VALMIN step roughly every OTHER wave, not every wave of any kind. Our spawner must bump them on plane waves only.
The ramp is play-mode-only (`BIT GAMODE / BPL 15$`), and both counters reset to 7/0 at game start — they are per-GAME, not persistent.

reasoning: The BIT/BVC decode is correct and is exactly the undecoded-operand class the brief warns about. NEWSHL (RBARON.MAC:2153-2161) verbatim: `NEWSHL: BIT SWSTAT ;FIRE BUTTON? / BPL NWSHL1 ;NO / LDA S.XFLG / EOR I,40 / STA S.XFLG / BIT GUN.ST / BVC 10$ / RTS / 10$: INC GUN.ST ;INC GUN USE COUNTER (OVERHEAT)`. 6502 BIT sets V <- operand bit 6; BVC branches when V is clear, i.e. proceeds to fire. So firing is blocked precisely when GUN.ST bit 6 is set. GUN.ST's only writers are this INC, the cool-down SBC (:2173-2177) and a clear at end-of-life (:1110) — nothing else can set bit 6 — so bit 6 first becomes set when the counter INCs 0x3F -> 0x40. Threshold = 64. Independently confirmed by the OVERHEAT message path at :5334-5337 (`BIT GUN.ST ;OVERHEATED GUNS ? / BVC 25$ / LDA I,16. / JSR MESSAGE`), the same bit-6 test. Cadence verified rather than assumed: NEWSHL has exactly ONE caller, MAIN:779 (`JSR NEWSHL ;GENERATE SHELLS`), and MAIN blocks in INTWAIT and loops (:926, :878) — so it runs once per CALCULATION frame (96 ms), not on the 250 Hz NMI (which carries only MODSND/SCOREM/PLPROP). 64 x 96 ms = 6.144 s. Ours: GUN_OVERHEAT_LIMIT = 30 (guns.ts:59), governing at :151 and :158, and guns.ts is live (imported by main.ts). 30 x 96 ms = 2.88 s. The divergence is real and the direction and magnitude are right.
corrections: IT IS 64 HELD-TRIGGER FRAMES, NOT 64 SHELLS. `INC GUN.ST` at :2161 executes BEFORE the free-slot search at NWSHL0 (:2163-2171), which RTSs when no slot is free — so heat accrues on every frame the trigger is held even when NO shell is actually spawned. The SHELLS pool is shared between player and enemy shells (the D7/D6 flags set at :2228-2232), so it genuinely can be full. Our guns.ts:151 returns early WITHOUT adding heat when the pool is full (`if (guns.overheated || guns.shells.length >= SHELL_SLOTS) return guns`) — that is a SECOND divergence in the same routine, and it must be fixed alongside the threshold or a full pool will silently make our gun cooler than the ROM's.

The finding's aside that our 30 'sits awkwardly close to SHELL_SLOTS = 13' understates the design constraint it names: guns.ts:56-57 explicitly justifies 30 as 'must exceed SHELL_SLOTS'. With the real threshold at 64 that constraint is satisfied with far more headroom, so no compensating change to SHELL_SLOTS is needed.
GUN.ST is also force-cleared at end-of-life (:1108-1110, `LDA I,0 / STA GUN.ST ;CLR OVERHEATED GUNS`) — a death cools the guns instantly. Ours has no such reset.

reasoning: Attacked specifically for a hidden persistence mechanism per the brief; there is none. The lockout is re-derived from GUN.ST every frame — the ONLY test is `BIT GUN.ST / BVC 10$ / RTS` (RBARON.MAC:2158-2160), executed fresh on each NEWSHL call, and I grepped every GUN.ST reference in RBARON.MAC (:175 storage, :1110 EOL clear, :2158 test, :2161 INC, :2173/:2177 cool, :5334 message test) — there is no latch byte, no 'overheated' flag, and no other gate on firing anywhere. Clearing path verified: NWSHL1 (:2172-2177) `SEC / LDA GUN.ST / SBC I,3 ;GUNS COOL 3 TIMES AS FAST AS HEAT / BCS 10$ / LDA I,0 / 10$: STA GUN.ST`, and it runs once per calc frame (inside NEWSHL, sole caller MAIN:779). Arithmetic: GUN.ST is pinned at exactly 0x40 = 64 (the RTS at :2160 precedes the INC at :2161, so it cannot climb past 64); one released frame gives 64 - 3 = 61 = 0x3D, bit 6 CLEAR, and the gun fires again on the very next frame — 96 ms later. The finding's two consequences both hold: hold the trigger and you jam FOREVER (held -> BIT SWSTAT branches away from the cooling path, and the RTS skips the INC, so GUN.ST neither cools nor climbs); tap it and you settle into ~3 shots per released frame. Ours (guns.ts:144-160) latches `overheated: true` at :158 and clears it only when heat reaches 0 (:148, `overheated: heat > 0 ? guns.overheated : false`), which from 30 takes ceil(30/3) = 10 released frames = 960 ms — a 10x-longer lockout, on top of CB-008's <half-count trigger. Both real.
corrections: The finding's proposed fix ('derive overheat from heat >= 64 at read time') is very slightly wrong as a bit-6 emulation. The ROM tests BIT 6, not a >= threshold. Because GUN.ST is clamped at 0x40 by the RTS-before-INC and cooling floors at 0, the two are equivalent IN PRACTICE for the player gun — but only because of that clamp. Port it as `(heat & 0x40) !== 0` if you want the ROM's actual semantics, or as `heat >= 64` with the pin at 64 explicitly preserved (i.e. do NOT let heat climb past 64). Do not implement `heat >= 64` while also letting heat keep rising, or the cool-down maths changes.

The finding calls the ROM's design 'a soft rate-limiter' and says holding the trigger jams you 'permanently' — both true, but note the jam is broken by end-of-life (:1110 clears GUN.ST) as well as by releasing the trigger. Not a gameplay escape, but the reset exists and ours lacks it.

'Ours takes ten [frames]' is right for a full 30-heat lockout, but our latch actually clears at heat == 0 from WHATEVER heat it holds — so the recovery is ceil(heat/3) frames, not a fixed ten. The 10x figure is the worst case, which is also the common case under sustained fire.

reasoning: The brief demanded the radix region be PROVEN; it is, decisively, and by more than the absence of a period. `S.MAXZ =19 ;SHELL MAX Z (* 100)` sits at RBARON.MAC:492, between `.RADIX 16` (:74) and the file's only decimal island (:6217-6280) — so 0x19 = 25. The surrounding equate block settles it beyond argument, because it uses BOTH conventions side by side and the comments cross-check them: `L.SHL =8` (:488); `N.SHL =12.*L.SHL ;13 SHELLS` (:489, EXPLICIT period where decimal is wanted); `GRLSHL =0E ;GROUND SHELL DB = 14. BYTES` (:490 — 0x0E = 14, hex proven by its own comment); `S.DPTH =100` (:493); `P.MAXR =1FF` (:471, unambiguously hex); `STINIT =18 ;SCORE 24.*4 MS.COUNT` (:507 — 0x18 = 24, hex proven by its own comment); `DRNPNT =30. ;300 POINTS` (:508, explicit period). S.MAXZ carries no period, so it is hex 0x19 = 25. The `&0FF` in the comparison is itself a proof: `CMP I,S.MAXZ&0FF` (:5218) masks the LOW byte — if S.MAXZ were the 16-bit 0x1900 the mask would yield 0 and the shell would die instantly. It is a byte-sized 0x19, and the comment's '(* 100)' means the compared byte is the Z MSB (scale x0x100). Birth verified: `LDA I,S.DPTH&0FF00/100 ;INITIALIZE Z (MSB) / STA AX,SHELLS+5` (:2235-2236) with S.DPTH = 0x100, so MSB = 1. Motion verified: PSTSHL (:5213-5227) `INC AX,SHELLS+5 ;UPDATE Z (DEPTH) / LDA AX,SHELLS+5 / CMP I,S.MAXZ&0FF / BCC 20$ / LDA I,0 / STA AX,SHELLS+7 ;FLAG CLEARED`. Sub-step count verified rather than assumed: SHLMOT (:5192) calls PSTSHL FOUR times (:5195, :5198, :5201, :5204 — the last a JMP tail-call), and SHLMOT itself is called once per calc frame from MAIN. So 4 increments per 96 ms frame. ROM flight = 25 - 1 = 24 increments = 6 calc frames = 576 ms. Ours (guns.ts:41) S_MAXZ = 19, born at z = 0, +1 per sub-step, expiring at z >= 19 (guns.ts:189/:201) = 19 increments = 4.75 -> 5 calc frames = 480 ms, i.e. 79% of the ROM's range. The finding's numbers and its proposed fix (25, shell born at z = 1) are both correct.
corrections: NOTE FOR THE IMPLEMENTER — the fix is 24 INCREMENTS, however you spell it. `S_MAXZ = 25` is only correct if the shell is ALSO born at z = 1 (as the finding says). If our shell keeps its z = 0 birth, the equivalent constant is 24, not 25. Changing S_MAXZ to 25 while leaving the birth at z = 0 gives 25 increments and overshoots the ROM by one sub-step.

S_MAXZ is not purely a range constant in our code — guns.ts:133 `depthToShellZ = (depth * S_MAXZ) / SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH` uses it as the depth->shell-Z PROJECTION scale, and guns.ts:222 uses that in the collision Z bound. Bumping S_MAXZ 19 -> 25 therefore also rescales the collision window unless SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH (800, itself inferred) is re-derived. The finding's 'fix is the constant' understates this: it is a one-line change with a second-order effect on hit detection that must be re-checked.
PSTSHL steps ALL shells, player and enemy alike (it walks the whole SHELLS pool on the SHELLS+7 active bit), so S.MAXZ is the enemy shell's range too — not just the player's gun reach.
